options.text: Write an email about a project delay
Write a professional email to my manager explaining that the Q2 report will be delayed because I'm waiting on data from finance

Write a professional email to my manager explaining that the Q2 report will be 3 days late due to waiting on data from the finance team. The tone should be apologetic but solution-focused. Include a brief explanation of the delay, acknowledge the impact, and mention next steps. Keep it concise, around 100-150 words.

responses.response_weak:
Subject: Project Delay

Hi,

I wanted to let you know that there will be a delay with the project. We're experiencing some issues that are causing us to fall behind schedule. I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

I'll keep you updated on our progress.

Thanks,
[Your name]
responses.response_strong:
Subject: Q2 Report Delay - 3 Days Behind Schedule

Hi [Manager's name],

I'm writing to inform you that the Q2 report will be delayed by 3 days from our original deadline. We're currently waiting on critical financial data from the finance team that's essential for completing the analysis.

I understand this delay may impact your planning for upcoming meetings and presentations, and I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience.

To move forward, I've already followed up with the finance team and confirmed they'll have the data to us by [specific date]. Once received, I'll prioritize completing the report and can have it ready by [new deadline date].

I'll send you a brief update tomorrow and notify you immediately once the report is complete.

Best regards,
[Your name]

weak.what_happened: Most people start with a question like this — it's a natural way to ask. The challenge is that when a request is this general, the AI gives a generic response that misses all the important details. It didn't mention the Q2 report, the 3-day delay, or the finance team issue. Compare that to the strong response, which included specific timelines, acknowledged impact, and outlined next steps — all because the prompt spelled out exactly what was needed.
weak.principle: Be specific, not vague — the more details you give an AI about what you want, the more useful and complete its response will be.
weak.tip: Instead of asking for 'an email about X,' include the key facts you want covered: who, what, when, why, and how you want it to sound. Think of it like briefing a colleague who needs to write the email for you.

medium.what_happened: Your prompt got you a decent email, but it lacked the specific details and structure that would make it truly professional. The AI gave you the basics but missed key elements like acknowledging impact, providing a timeline, and outlining next steps because you didn't explicitly ask for these components.
medium.principle: Show what 'good' looks like by describing the specific elements, tone, and structure you want in your output.
medium.tip: When asking AI to write something important, describe not just the content but also the structure you want. Say things like 'include an apology, explain next steps, and keep it under 150 words' rather than just describing the basic situation.

strong.what_happened: You picked the strongest option and got a complete, professional email that your manager could actually use. The weak prompt produced a vague message that left your manager guessing about what project, how long the delay would be, and what happens next. Your detailed prompt gave the AI everything it needed to write something specific and actionable.
strong.principle: When you give the AI specific details about what you want (tone, length, key points to include), it can craft a response that matches your exact needs instead of guessing.
strong.tip: Next time you need to write something important, tell the AI the specific tone you want, roughly how long it should be, and list the key points that must be included. This works for emails, presentations, reports, or any written communication.
